channel.title: Daily AI Digest
channel.description: Curated AI/ML signals — zero LLMs, pure algorithmic curation

item.title: OcclusionFormer: Arranging Z-Order for Layout-Grounded Image Generation
item.description: 📄 New in cs.CV — Recent layout-to-image models have achieved remarkable progress in spatial controllability. However, they still struggle with inter-object occlusion. When bounding boxes overlap, most existing methods lack explicit occlusion information, which makes the generation in intersection regions inherent...

item.title: Uni-Edit: Intelligent Editing Is A General Task For Unified Model Tuning
item.description: 📄 New in cs.CV — Currently, enhancing Unified Multimodal Models (UMMs) with image understanding, generation, and editing capabilities mainly relies on mixed multi-task training. Due to inherent task conflicts, such strategy requires complex multi-stage pipelines, massive data mixing, and balancing tricks, merely ...

item.title: You Only Need Minimal RLVR Training: Extrapolating LLMs via Rank-1 Trajectories
item.description: 📄 New in cs.LG, cs.CL — Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) has become a dominant paradigm for improving reasoning in large language models (LLMs), yet the underlying geometry of the resulting parameter trajectories remains underexplored. In this work, we demonstrate that RLVR weight trajectories are e...

item.title: Mem-$π$: Adaptive Memory through Learning When and What to Generate
item.description: 📄 New in cs.CL, cs.AI — We present Mem-$π$, a framework for adaptive memory in large language model (LLM) agents, where useful guidance is generated on demand rather than retrieved from external memory stores. Existing memory-augmented agents typically rely on similarity-based retrieval from episodic memory banks or ski...

item.title: SpecBench: Measuring Reward Hacking in Long-Horizon Coding Agents
item.description: 📄 New in cs.SE, cs.AI — As long-horizon coding agents produce more code than any developer can review, oversight collapses onto a single surface: the automated test suite. Reward hacking naturally arises in this setup, as the agent optimizes for passing tests while deviating from the users true goal. We study this rewar...

item.title: Variance Reduction for Expectations with Diffusion Teachers
item.description: 📄 New in cs.LG, cs.AI — Pretrained diffusion models serve as frozen teachers feeding downstream pipelines such as text-to-3D, single-step distillation, and data attribution. The teacher gradients these pipelines consume are Monte Carlo (MC) expectations over noise levels and Gaussian noise samples; their estimator varia...

item.title: Equilibrium Reasoners: Learning Attractors Enables Scalable Reasoning
item.description: 📄 New in cs.LG — Scaling test-time compute by iteratively updating a latent state has emerged as a powerful paradigm for reasoning. Yet the internal mechanisms that enable these iterative models to generalize beyond memorized patterns remain unclear. We hypothesize that generalizable reasoning arises from learnin...

item.title: BigBodyCobain / Shadowbroker
item.description: 💬 Major HN discussion (123 comments) · ⭐ 327 GitHub stars today — Open-source intelligence for the global theater. Track everything from the corporate/private jets of the wealthy, and spy satellites, to seismic events in one unified interface. Hook an AI agent up to have it parse through data and find previously unseen correlations. The knowledge is available t...

item.title: karpathy / autoresearch
item.description: ⭐ 367 GitHub stars today · ⭐ 82.4k total stars — AI agents running research on single-GPU nanochat training automatically

item.title: unslothai / unsloth
item.description: ⭐ 147 GitHub stars today · ⭐ 64.9k total stars — Unsloth Studio is a web UI for training and running open models like Gemma 4, Qwen3.6, DeepSeek, gpt-oss locally.
